data.title: Waymo to expand services to Miami with Uber-backed Moove
data.description:
Alphabet's (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) autonomous vehicle driving unit Waymo is expanding its operations into Miami, the company said in a blog post Thursday.

Through its new fleet partnership with Moove, Waymo expects to work with them first in Phoenix and put them in charge for managing fleet operations, facilities, and charging infrastructure.

Waymo said it will continue to be responsible for driver validation and operation and offer the service through its existing app.

"We are excited to partner with Moove in Phoenix and later Miami, bringing together their mobility-focused fleet management experience with our growing Waymo One service," Waymo's VP of operations Ryan McNamara said.

Moove is a mobility fintech company that helps with the financing of vehicle ownership. The company has a global partnership with Uber (UBER), which led a $100 million series B funding round for the African firm. It has operations in 12 markets across Africa, the Middle East, Europe, Asia, and Latin America.

Uber and rival ride-hailing service firm Lyft (LYFT) are down 5% reacting to the announcement of the news.

data.title: Interpublic sells digital company Huge
data.description:
• Advertising and marketing solutions provider Interpublic Group (NYSE:IPG) has sold the digital company Huge to AEA Investors, a private investment firm.
  • In connection with the transaction, Huge will partner with AEA's existing portfolio company, Hero Digital.
  • The terms of the deal were not disclosed.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Apple set to invest $1B in manufacturing plant in Indonesia, investment minister says
data.description:
Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) is looking to invest $1 billion in a manufacturing plant in Indonesia, the country's investment minister Rosan Roeslani told reporters.

The news comes a couple of days after Indonesia reportedly received an increased investment offer of $1 billion from the tech giant, as the latter looks to reverse the ban on iPhone 16 sales in the country.

In October, Indonesia had restricted Apple (AAPL) from selling its new iPhone 16 series in the country, noting that the company has yet to meet local investment requirements.

Roeslani added the details of the investment are still being finalized, the report said.

The iPhone-marker had previously proposed to invest around $100M over two years in Indonesia. Prior to that, the firm had offered to invest around $10M in Indonesia, which included investment in a factory in Bandung that makes accessories and components.

The agreement could be finalized within one week, Roeslani said.

data.title: Algorhythm Holdings shares tumble on pricing $9.5M public offering
data.description:
• Algorhythm Holdings (NASDAQ:RIME) has priced a public offering with gross proceeds of ~$9.5M.
  • The offering comprises 55.88M shares of the company’s common stock.
  • Each share of common stock or pre-funded warrant will be sold with one Series A Warrant to purchase one share of common stock at an exercise price of $0.17 per share and one Series B Warrant to purchase one share of common stock at an exercise price of $0.34 per share.
  • The Series A Warrants will expire on the five-year anniversary of its initial exercise date, and the Series B Warrants will expire on the two and one-half-year anniversary of its initial exercise date.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds from this offering for working capital and other general corporate purposes, and for repayment of certain outstanding senior secured notes of the company.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about December 6, 2024.
  • RIME shares fell over 45% on Thursday.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Mali issues arrest warrant for Barrick Gold CEO Bristow - Reuters
data.description:
Mali's government has issued an arrest warrant for Barrick Gold (NYSE:GOLD) CEO Mark Bristow, Reuters reported Thursday, in the latest escalation of tensions after four senior local employees of the company were arrested in September.

Last week, four additional employees of Barrick's (GOLD) Loulo-Gounkoto mining complex in Mali were charged and detained pending trial.

Tensions between Mali, one of Africa's biggest gold producers, and miners operating in the country have surged after the country revised its mining code with the aim of obtaining more revenues from the companies.

Bristow is accused of money laundering and violation of financial regulations, according to the warrant document dated December 2 which was seen by Reuters.

data.title: Eventbrite announces chief operating officer Lanny Baker to depart company
data.description:
• Eventbrite (NYSE:EB) announced that chief operating officer Lanny Baker plans to depart from the company, effective December 31, 2024.
  • The company also reaffirmed its financial outlook for the fourth quarter and full year of 2024.
  • EB shares were down 2.2% on Thursday.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Boeing plans to cut 141 workers in Florida, including at Kennedy Space Center
data.description:
Boeing (NYSE:BA) plans to send layoff notices to more than 100 employees in Florida starting next month, according to a Worker Adjustment and Retraining Notification Act letter.

The affected locations include Titusville and the Kennedy Space Center. The company in 2023 spent more than $1 billion among suppliers in Florida.

Boeing (BA) attributed the job cuts to its need to "align workforce levels with financial realities and a more focused set of priorities," according to a statement to the Orlando Business Journal.

The company in October announced plans to fire approximately 17,000 employees amid efforts to reduce expenses. The cuts came after a strike brought key operations to a standstill.

data.title: Strawberry Fields REIT prices public offering of common stock
data.description:
• Strawberry Fields REIT (NYSE:STRW) priced an underwritten public offering of 3.3M shares of its common stock for total gross proceeds of approximately $35 million.
  • The offering is expected to close on December 6, 2024, subjected to customary closing conditions.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Sphere Entertainment falls amid Hedgeye short call
data.description:
Sphere Entertainment (NYSE:SPHR) dropped 2.5% amid a short call from Hedgeye, which sees the potential for 30% downside.

"SPHR faces an array of challenges that we expect will weigh on growth through 2025 and potentially beyond," Hedgeye analyst Andrew Freedman wrote in a note earlier on Thursday. "Without significant growth in the Sphere Experience segment to offset persistently high operating costs, profitability remains elusive."

"We have serious doubts about the sustainability of reducing show volumes as a long-term strategy to maintain higher utilization rates and grow revenue," Freedman added.

Freedman also highlights questions surrounding MSG Networks and the unresolved term loan issues.

Sphere Entertainment (SPHR) has short interest of 29.5%.

data.title: Amgen to spend $1B on second NC manufacturing plant
data.description:
• Amgen (NASDAQ:AMGN) is planning to spend $1B to establish a second manufacturing facility in Holly Springs, North Carolina.
  • The move will bring its total investment in the Holly Springs area to $1.55B.
  • Amgen said it intends to make North Carolina an important part of its global manufacturing network. The investments are expected to create around 370 new jobs in the area.
